</script><script id="__NEXT_DATA__" type="application/json">{"props":{"pageProps":{"_sentryTraceData":"05a8fae3d9d64627b15a3a2ee6e2de4b-b63981b52446429f-0","_sentryBaggage":"sentry-environment=production,sentry-release=odsUSnXHa7Xks8o0SB39f,sentry-public_key=96a2ce264cf34a8eb5bbd664e8a614e8,sentry-trace_id=05a8fae3d9d64627b15a3a2ee6e2de4b,sentry-sample_rate=0.01,sentry-transaction=%2Finsurance-coverage,sentry-sampled=false","data":{"id":19081,"date":"2015-10-19T15:36:03","modified":"2024-12-24T16:56:02","slug":"insurance-coverage","type":"page","link":"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage","title":{"rendered":"Insurance Coverage for Rehab: Does Insurance Cover Rehab?"},"content":{"rendered":"\u003cdiv\u003e\u003ch2\u003eDoes Your Health Insurance Cover Substance Use Treatment?\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eYes, health insurance plans will generally cover the cost of treatment for substance use disorder (drug and \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol\"\u003ealcohol\u003c/a\u003e addiction) and mental health conditions. The particular health insurance plan that you have will determine how much of your treatment is covered by your insurance plan, as well as how much you will be required to pay out-of-pocket. It’s best to check with your insurance provider before trying to enter a treatment program for substance abuse issues to understand the details of what is covered under your plan.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eFind out instantly if your insurance may be able to cover all or part of the cost of addiction treatment. Or call us today at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a753a81aed9\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e You will speak with an admissions navigator, and they can help you through the verification process. American Addiction Centers can take the confusion out of contacting your insurance provider directly. Simply call us or fill in the form below and we can communicate with your insurance directly. By filling in our confidential form below, we can find out which treatment centers are in-network, we can advise you on the length of stay covered, and we can save you time and from the hassle of contacting your insurance company and/or looking through hard to understand insurance documents.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81afaf\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"\u003cstrong\u003eHealth Insurance Glossary of Terms\u003c\\/strong\u003e\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eBefore you continue reading, here are some terms and definitions that may help you better understand your insurance policy and substance abuse treatment coverage.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDeductible\u003c\\/strong\u003e: The amount you pay for health care services before insurance coverage kicks in. If you have a $2,000 deductible, you pay the first $2,000, and then your insurance pays the rest. Sometimes you\\u2019ll have a copayment or will need to pay for coinsurance for covered services after the deductible is met.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCopayment\u003c\\/strong\u003e: The fixed amount you pay for health care services after the deductible is reached. For example, your copayment may be $20 per doctor visit.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCoinsurance\u003c\\/strong\u003e: The percentage of costs you pay for health care services received after you\\u2019ve met your deductible.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOut-of-Network\u003c\\/strong\u003e: A health care provider or organization that does not have an agreement with your health insurance provider.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIn-Network\u003c\\/strong\u003e \u003cstrong\u003eProvider\u003c\\/strong\u003e. A healthcare provider or organization that has an agreement with your insurance provider to provide care at a discounted rate.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOut-of-pocket and out-of-pocket limit\u003c\\/strong\u003e: Out-of-pocket expenses are those you will be financially responsible for paying on your own. The out-of-pocket limit is the most you will have to pay for services in a year.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePremium:\u003c\\/strong\u003e The amount of money you must pay (monthly or annually) for your health insurance policy.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003eInsurance Providers and Rehab Coverage\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eHealth insurance benefits are designed to make health care both affordable and accessible, and there’s no stigma attached to asking for help. People with addictions and insurance should use their coverage to the fullest in order to get the care they need to leave addictions behind for good. Talking to plan administrators is a great place to start, but remember that the staff of addiction treatment facilities can also be of vital help.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIn some cases, they can smooth the path to payment, so families have one less thing to worry over as they recover. Explore more information about insurance providers and rehab coverage below. Call us at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a753a81afda\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e If you do not see your provider listed below, we may still be able to work with you or your insurance provider to find treatment.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion-links\" id=\"portal_accordion-links-insurance\" data-title=\"\" data-type=\"insurance\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"type\":\"insurance\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"flex-grid-69a753a81b00c\" style=\"\"\u003e\n\t\t\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv style=\"flex: 1; text-align: ;\"\u003e\n\t\t\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazyel\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-115447 size-full\" alt=\"\" width=\"500\" height=\"334\" sizes=\"(max-width: 500px) 100vw, 500px\" data-src=\"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/doctor-and-clipboard.jpg\" src=\"data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20500%20334'%3E%3C/svg%3E\" data-srcset=\"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/doctor-and-clipboard.jpg 500w, https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/doctor-and-clipboard-300x200.jpg 300w\"\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\n\t\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\n\u003cdiv style=\"flex: 1; text-align: ;\"\u003e\n\t\t\n\u003ch2\u003eOptions With and Without Insurance\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b1c9\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"What Types of Rehab Does Insurance Cover?\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eThere are various types of addiction treatment in the United States, most of which are often covered by health insurance policies. Nearly 90 percent of Americans have some form of health insurance in 2021.\u003csup\u003e1\u003c\\/sup\u003e\\u00a0Depending on your insurance plan specifics, addiction treatment programs covered by insurance include:\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca title=\\\"Inpatient Care\\\" href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/inpatient\\\"\u003eInpatient rehabilitation.\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca title=\\\"Outpatient Treatment\\\" href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/outpatient-treatment\\\"\u003eOutpatient care\u003c\\/a\u003e.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/intensive-outpatient-programs\\\"\u003eIntensive outpatient.\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca title=\\\"Medical Detox\\\" href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/drug-detox\\\"\u003eMedical detoxification.\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eMedication-assisted treatment.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca title=\\\"Co-Occurring Mental Health Conditions\\\" href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/co-occurring-disorders\\\"\u003eDual Diagnosis \u0026amp; Co-occurring mental health treatments\u003c\\/a\u003e.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eContinuing care (e.g., counseling, therapy)\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eMaintenance medication to support ongoing sobriety \u0026amp; other forms of\\u00a0\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/addiction-medications\\\"\u003eMAT.\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b205\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"Going to Rehab Without Insurance\"},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e If you don\\u2019t have private health insurance, don\\u2019t let this be a barrier to accessing treatment when you are ready. There are still various free rehab and state-funded rehab options available to you. States offer financial assistance for those who have no insurance and are in need free or low-cost addiction treatment. Google your state, county, or city to find low-cost substance use disorder programs and services. Many states offer free rehab or have state-funded rehab options, state insurance policies, and other ways to help those who need it.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eOther options for paying for drug and alcohol rehab include:\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eLoans and\\/or payment plans. Many treatment centers provide financing options that allow you to pay back the cost of treatment in small increments after treatment.\u003cbr \\/\u003e\\nScholarships. Some treatment facilities offer scholarships to those in need to cover all or part of the cost. Some states also offer scholarships and grants to those with addiction who need help paying for treatment.\u003cbr \\/\u003e\\nFinancial support from family. Though asking for help can be difficult, you may be surprised by how many people may want to support you. Ask family and friends for financial support in your endeavor to get sober. It\\u2019s worth a try.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b227\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"The Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) and Health Insurance Coverage\"},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e In March 2010, the Affordable Care Act was signed into law to make health insurance affordable and accessible to more people.2 The law also aimed to expand the Medicaid program and support innovative medical care delivery methods.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eThe Affordable Care Act also states that no one can be denied access to health insurance, even if they have a preexisting condition like substance use disorder. The ACA lists substance use disorder as one of the 10 elements of essential health benefits that all healthcare insurers must provide. This means that all health insurance providers are required to provide coverage for mental health care and treatment.3 Both rehab for substance use disorder\\/addiction and mental health care services fall under this mandate.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\u003cbr\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\u003cstyle\u003e\n\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b00c {\n\t\tdisplay: block;\n\t\tmargin: 15px 0;\n\t}\n\t@media screen and (min-width: 1100px) {\n\t\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b00c {\n\t\t\tdisplay: flex;\n\t\t\tflex-direction: ;\n\t\t\talign-items: ;\n\t\t\tmargin: 15px 0 30px;\n\t\t}\n\t\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b00c \u003e div {\n\t\t\tmargin: 0 15px;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t\u003c/style\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"flex-grid-69a753a81b256\" style=\"\"\u003e\n\t\t\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv style=\"flex: 1; text-align: ;\"\u003e\n\t\t\n\u003ch2\u003eCommon Types of Healthcare Plans and Benefits\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThe three most common healthcare plans are health maintenance organization (HMO) plans, preferred provider organization (PPO) plans, and point of sale (POS) plans.4 Substance abuse treatment and recovery may be covered by your insurance provider. Learn more about which plan, HMO or PPO, offers the best coverage:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b2ee\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"HMO (Health Maintenance Organization)\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/insurance-coverage\\/hmo\\\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHMO (Health Maintenance Organization)\u003c\\/strong\u003e\u003c\\/a\u003e\\u00a0plans allow patients to choose their primary care physician and see that doctor for most of their medical needs. This allows them to form a relationship with a doctor who knows their whole health history. When seeking a specialist or physician outside of the network with an HMO plan, a referral is needed by your primary care physician.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c\\/sup\u003e\\u00a0HMOs have lower or no deductibles and overall coverage is usually a lower cost than PPO.\u003csup\u003e6\u003c\\/sup\u003e\\u00a0Pros of HMO coverage are for those that are not seeking a specialist and healthcare providers out of their network and paying lower premiums.\u003csup\u003e6\\u00a0\u003c\\/sup\u003eHealth maintenance organizations cover healthcare costs for all in-network providers and services. You will be responsible for paying out-of-pocket if you seek care with a provider that is out-of-network with your insurance provider. HMOs generally offer lower monthly premiums and deductibles with set copays.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b340\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"PPO (Preferred Provider Organization)\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/insurance-coverage\\/ppo\\\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePPO (Preferred Provider Organization)\u003c\\/strong\u003e\u003c\\/a\u003e\\u00a0plans allow patients to see healthcare providers in and out of their network without referrals.\u003csup\u003e7\u003c\\/sup\u003e\\u00a0Preferred provider organization plans offer a little more flexibility in that they allow members to visit any doctor of their choosing, both in and out of network, but you\\u2019ll pay less if you stay in-network. You don\\u2019t need a primary care physician, and referrals are not required to see a specialist. PPO plans have higher monthly premiums and lower copays for treatments and services received. PPOs can have higher deductibles than those with an HMO plan.\u003csup\u003e6\u003c\\/sup\u003e\\u00a0One of the pros of PPO coverage is having the option see specialists and other healthcare providers outside of your network without a referral from your primary care physician.\u003csup\u003e6\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b362\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"POS (Point of Sale)\"},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePOS (Point of Sale):\u003c\\/strong\u003e\\u00a0Point of sale plans are similar to HMO plans. The primary difference is that you can sometimes see care providers outside of your network in certain cases. This is what \\u201cpoint of services\\u201d means. Each insurance company varies with its rules on POS plans, so be sure to check the details of your specific insurer if this is the type of plan you have.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg loading=\"lazyel\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-64637 size-full\" alt=\"\" width=\"500\" height=\"249\" sizes=\"(max-width: 500px) 100vw, 500px\" data-src=\"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/hmo-ppo-chart-1-e1724970556772.png\" src=\"data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20500%20249'%3E%3C/svg%3E\" data-srcset=\"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/hmo-ppo-chart-1-e1724970556772.png 500w, https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/hmo-ppo-chart-1-e1724970556772-300x149.png 300w\"\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\u003cstyle\u003e\n\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b256 {\n\t\tdisplay: block;\n\t\tmargin: 15px 0;\n\t}\n\t@media screen and (min-width: 1100px) {\n\t\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b256 {\n\t\t\tdisplay: flex;\n\t\t\tflex-direction: ;\n\t\t\talign-items: ;\n\t\t\tmargin: 15px 0 30px;\n\t\t}\n\t\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b256 \u003e div {\n\t\t\tmargin: 0 15px;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t\u003c/style\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"flex-grid-69a753a81b384\" style=\"\"\u003e\n\t\t\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv style=\"flex: 1; text-align: ;\"\u003e\n\t\t\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cimg loading=\"lazyel\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone size-full wp-image-115446\" alt=\"\" width=\"500\" height=\"359\" sizes=\"(max-width: 500px) 100vw, 500px\" data-src=\"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/health-insurance.jpg\" src=\"data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20500%20359'%3E%3C/svg%3E\" data-srcset=\"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/health-insurance.jpg 500w, https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/health-insurance-300x215.jpg 300w\"\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv style=\"flex: 1; text-align: ;\"\u003e\n\t\t\n\u003ch2\u003eCost and Coverage\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b47d\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"Drug Addiction Coverage and Cost\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eMany healthcare insurance providers may be able to cover all or part of the\\u00a0\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/rehab-cost\\\"\u003ecost of alcohol or drug rehab\u003c\\/a\u003e. The cost of rehab varies depending on the patient\\u2019s level of treatment, length of stay, \u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/insurance-coverage\\/anthem-blue-cross-blue-shield-colorado\\\"\u003einsurance coverage\u003c\\/a\u003e, and other unique factors. The amount of coverage or insurance acceptance is based on the insurance policy that each patient is covered by. This means out-of-pocket expenses will vary.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eHealthCare.gov reports that plans participating in the insurance marketplace must provide care in 10 essential health categories, one of those being addiction care. Many private health insurance plans follow these same rules, too, so they could be sold on the marketplace at a later point in time. Most insurance policies don\\u2019t separate drugs into \\u201ccovered\\u201d and \\u201cnon-covered\\u201d categories. If addiction treatments are considered a covered benefit, then care is provided to anyone who has an addiction, regardless of what that addiction is caused by. This is the same model health insurance programs use in order to treat other medical conditions.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eFor example, some people develop obesity through overeating, while others develop weight difficulties due to hormonal or gland abnormalities. Health insurance programs that provide a weight-loss benefit don\\u2019t cover one type of weight loss while eliminating the other. Doing so would make the plans much more expensive, as underwriters would need to delve deep into the medical history of each person needing care, and the plans would need to cover sophisticated tests in order to determine how the obesity came about. By just covering all obesity treatment, the plan can save money.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eThe same is true for addictions. Plan administrators don\\u2019t want to cover in-depth testing and interviews about what drugs were used, how the drugs were developed, where they came from, and what they\\u2019re mixed with. Plans can keep things simple by covering all drugs, if they provide an addiction care benefit.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eCovering addiction care can also help states to keep costs down. For example, a PBS report suggests that incarcerating an adult for one year can cost up to $37,000, while providing residential care for addiction costs just $14,600. State-run plans might very well provide robust addiction care for all drugs simply because doing so could keep other costs in line.\u003cbr \\/\u003e\\nPrivate plans might see benefits if addicted people don\\u2019t land in expensive emergency rooms due to addictions or\\u00a0\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/overdose\\\"\u003eoverdoses\u003c\\/a\u003e. By providing care for addictions, they might also reduce the number of organ transplants they might need to cover. When it comes to savings, robust care for all drugs might be the best way to go, and many insurance plans do just that.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eThat means anyone with questions about what drugs are and aren\\u2019t covered by insurance can just look at this page and get clear answers in just minutes. That page is a good first stop for anyone with addiction treatment questions.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b4b4\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"What Mental Health Issues Are Covered by Insurance?\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eThe legislation passed as part of the Affordable Care Act didn\\u2019t stop with addiction care. Plans were also required, as part of the legislation, to provide the same level of care for mental health concerns that they do for physical health concerns. That means plans that provide doctor visits for a foot problem for $20 must also provide doctor visits for depression for $20. The care, and the cost, must be the same. The\\u00a0\u003ca title=\\\"American Psychological Association\\\" href=\\\"https:\\/\\/www.apa.org\\/helpcenter\\/parity-guide\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" rel=\\\"noopener noreferrer\\\"\u003eAmerican Psychological Association\u003c\\/a\u003e\\u00a0says these parity laws apply to all sorts of programs, including those provided by employers, those coming through health care exchanges, and those coming through Medicaid and CHIP.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eParity laws don\\u2019t explicitly state what sorts of mental health conditions plans must provide care for, but the rules are similar to those seen in drug addiction. If plans provide care for mental illness, they typically don\\u2019t specify that some illnesses are covered while others are not. That would require a huge amount of paperwork and time, and most plan administrators don\\u2019t have much of either of those things to spare, so the plans simply cover all of it. Again, this is an issue that\\u2019s best discussed with a plan administrator. But in general, fears that mental health issues won\\u2019t be covered because they\\u2019re \\u201cbad\\u201d are typically groundless. Health insurance just doesn\\u2019t work that way.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b52d\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"Are Treatment Medications Covered?\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eMaintenance programs, part of\\u00a0\u003ca title=\\\"Aftercare Treatment\\\" href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/aftercare\\\"\u003eaftercare treatment\u003c\\/a\u003e, are designed for people who can\\u2019t get to a normal level of functioning in the absence of drugs, even if they\\u2019ve been through a rehab program at a treatment center. Chemical alterations caused by drugs are just too severe in these people, and they need medications in order to correct those imbalances so they can live a life that\\u2019s free of the influence of drugs.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eMaintenance treatment helps many people in recovery from certain substance use disorders, often long after the initial rehabilitation stay. For instance, opioid maintenance therapy on an opioid agonist medication like methadone or buprenorphine can help prevent withdrawal, minimize cravings and, over the longer-term, help to discourage relapse and allow that person to focus on sobriety and other day-to-day obligations.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eMaintenance medications like Suboxone, buprenorphine, and Antabuse are made for people with these sorts of issues, but they can be expensive. Thankfully, most experts suggest that insurance plans typically do cover these drugs.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eThe National Alliance of Advocates for Buprenorphine Treatment says, for example, that most health insurance plans cover maintenance drugs for people in recovery from addictions to heroin and other similar drugs. The organization doesn\\u2019t specify how many plans offer this coverage or what typical copayments might be, but the group seems confident that most plans do provide this benefit.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eThe\\u00a0\u003ca title=\\\"Substance Abuse and Mental Health Service Administration\\\" href=\\\"https:\\/\\/www.samhsa.gov\\/medication-assisted-treatment\\/become-buprenorphine-waivered-practitioner\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" rel=\\\"noopener noreferrer\\\"\u003eSubstance Abuse and Mental Health Service Administration\u003c\\/a\u003e\\u00a0says, on the other hand, that\\u00a0\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/medicare\\\"\u003eMedicare\u003c\\/a\u003e\\u00a0and Medicaid plans will only cover these medications if their use is deemed vital for the ongoing health of the person in recovery. If the person is deemed somehow capable of healing without the medications, coverage wouldn\\u2019t be provided under these plans.\u003cbr \\/\u003e\\nThese two opposing views make it clear that plans can handle addiction maintenance care very differently. That\\u2019s why it pays for families in need of this care to ask about coverage, copayments, and therapies before they start to get them. Some programs may have extensive coverage while others do not. \u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_accordion\" id=\"portal_accordion-69a753a81b56f\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"The Cost of Rehab vs. The Cost of Addiction\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eAddiction comes at a great cost: to the individual, family, friends, the community, and society at large. There is a great cost related to lost productivity, premature death, and crime.\u003csup\u003e8\u003c\\/sup\u003e\\u00a0In fact, the societal cost of addiction is more than $532 billion a year.\u003csup\u003e9\u003c\\/sup\u003e\\u00a0But more important than money is that treatment saves lives. If you or your loved one are ready to take the brave step to get treatment, the time is now.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eWhether you have full insurance coverage for drug and\\u00a0\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/alcohol\\/rehab-treatment\\\"\u003ealcohol addiction treatment\u003c\\/a\u003e\\u00a0or no insurance at all, getting treatment is worth it. With the right treatment plan, you can begin your path to recovery and long-term sobriety.\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\n\u003cp\u003e\n\t\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\t\u003cstyle\u003e\n\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b384 {\n\t\tdisplay: block;\n\t\tmargin: 15px 0;\n\t}\n\t@media screen and (min-width: 1100px) {\n\t\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b384 {\n\t\t\tdisplay: flex;\n\t\t\tflex-direction: ;\n\t\t\talign-items: ;\n\t\t\tmargin: 15px 0 30px;\n\t\t}\n\t\t.flex-grid-69a753a81b384 \u003e div {\n\t\t\tmargin: 0 15px;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t\u003c/style\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a753a81b68b\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"Useful Health Insurance Resources\"},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli style=\\\"list-style-type: none;\\\"\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli style=\\\"list-style-type: none;\\\"\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/insurance-coverage\\/va-benefits\\\"\u003eVA Benefits \u0026amp; Insurance for Rehab\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca title=\\\"US. However, the extent to which your insurance will cover drug or alcohol rehab depends upon a variety of factors, including your policy’s particular behavioral health benefits, your rehab treatment provider, your particular needs, and more. Find the answer to whether your addiction treatment may be covered by your health insurance policy, along with answers to other relevant questions, below.","rehab-guide-learn":false,"hide_global_ctas":{"next_steps_nav":false,"top_banner":false,"pre_footer":false,"sticky_footer":false},"faqs":{"include_faq_schema":true,"faqs_list":[{"question":"How to bill insurance for substance abuse treatment?","answer":"People with addictions and insurance should use their coverage to the fullest in order to get the care they need. Talking to your insurance plan administrator by calling the number on the back of your insurance card is a great place to start. The staff of addiction treatment facilities can also be of vital help, easing the path to payment so families have one less thing to worry over as they recover."},{"question":"How to get financial assistance for substance abuse treatment?","answer":"Financial assistance for substance abuse treatment can come from federal, state, and local governments. There are also grants available through SAMHSA, as well as insurance options for certain populations, such as veterans or low-income families. \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/public-assistance\"\u003eRead more\u003c/a\u003e."},{"question":"What is the cost of outpatient substance abuse treatment?","answer":"Outpatient treatment for substance abuse can cover many different services and levels of care. It is recommended that patients work with their insurance provider and the rehab facility directly to determine their out-of-pocket expenses. \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost\"\u003eRead more\u003c/a\u003e."},{"question":"Can I Get or Change My Health Insurance Coverage After a Qualifying Life Event?","answer":"Certain life changes, referred to as qualifying life events, allow you to make changes to existing coverage or sign up for a new health insurance plan. These life changes include loss of health coverage, changes in your household, changes in your residence, and more. Qualifying life events include:\r\n\r\n\u003cul\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eLosing existing job-based, individual, or student health coverage.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eLosing eligibility for Medicare, Medicaid, or Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP).\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eTurning 26 years old and losing health coverage under your parent’s insurance plan.\r\nGetting married or divorced.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eHaving a baby or adopting a child.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eA death in your family that affects your health coverage.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eMoving to a different zip code or county that changes your health plan area.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eMoving as a student to or from the place where you will go to school.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eMoving to or from the place where you live and work as a seasonal worker.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eMoving to or from a shelter or other transitional housing.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eExperiencing changes in your income that affect your qualifying coverage.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eGaining membership in a federally recognized tribe or status as an Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA) Corporation shareholder.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eBecoming a U.S. citizen.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eLeaving incarceration such as jail or prison.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eStarting or ending service as an AmeriCorps member.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003c/ul\u003e\r\n\r\nAll situations are different, so these life events might require documentation—such as birth certificates, adoption records, marriage licenses, divorce papers, death certificates, rental agreements, or mortgages—to show that you have added family members, lost family members who provided the health coverage, or moved into a new health insurance plan area. These changes usually need to be done within 60 days of the life event, but check with you insurer on specifics."},{"question":"Can I Have Multiple Insurance Providers?","answer":"Yes, you can have multiple insurance providers. When two (or more) health insurance plan providers cover the healthcare costs of one person, this is called coordination of benefits. You may have dual insurance coverage if:\r\n\r\n\u003cul\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eYou are married and covered under your insurance plan and your spouse’s.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eYou are under 26 years old and covered by your parents’ insurance and your own.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\u003cli\u003eYou are under 26 years old with divorced parents and are under both parents’ plan as a dependent.\u003c/li\u003e\r\n\r\nYou are over 65 years old and have coverage through your employer and Medicare.\r\nIf you have two separate health insurance plans, one plan will be your primary coverage and the other is your secondary coverage. Your primary provider pays first – up to its coverage limits. Your secondary insurance will then step in and pay the remaining cost (partial or full) of the treatment(s). Even after secondary insurance pays, you may still have some out-of-pocket costs."}]},"page_level_pills":{"":null,"hardcoded_pills":{"include_hardcoded_pills":true,"custom_pick_hardcoded_pills":false,"custom_picked_hardcoded_pills":[]},"include_custom_segment_pills":true,"custom_pills":{"include_custom_pills":false,"custom_pills":false}}},"_links":{"self":[{"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/pages/19081"}],"collection":[{"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/pages"}],"about":[{"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/types/page"}],"author":[{"embeddable":true,"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/users/33"}],"replies":[{"embeddable":true,"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/comments?post=19081"}],"version-history":[{"count":20,"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/pages/19081/revisions"}],"predecessor-version":[{"id":120288,"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/pages/19081/revisions/120288"}],"wp:featuredmedia":[{"embeddable":true,"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/media/57932"}],"wp:attachment":[{"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/media?parent=19081"}],"wp:term":[{"taxonomy":"post_tag","embeddable":true,"href":"https://admin.americanaddictioncenters.org/wp-json/wp/v2/tags?post=19081"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"https://api.w.org/{rel}","templated":true}]}}},"ipAddress":"47.158.106.219","blockingData":{"menuItems":[{"ID":82513,"menu_order":1,"menu_item_parent":"0","title":"Treatment","url":"#","slug":"#","target":""},{"ID":82525,"menu_order":2,"menu_item_parent":"82513","title":"Levels Insurance Coverage for Rehab: Does Insurance Cover Rehab?

 
 
 
 Medical Detox 
 Insurance Accepted 
 Same Day Admission 

 Written by: Lindsay Curtis 

 Edited by: Amelia Sharp 

Updated Dec 24, 2024

 Step 1 /4 Check Coverage 

* These fields are required
 

 Find Insurance Carrier * 

Aetna

 
 
 

 Policy Membership ID 

 WXY1030Z0 
 
 
 
 

 

 Next 

 

 
 Your information is secure & protected by HIPAA 

 

 
 

 

Does Your Health Insurance Cover Substance Use Treatment?

Yes, health insurance plans will generally cover the cost of treatment for substance use disorder (drug and alcohol addiction) and mental health conditions. The particular health insurance plan that you have will determine how much of your treatment is covered by your insurance plan, as well as how much you will be required to pay out-of-pocket. It’s best to check with your insurance provider before trying to enter a treatment program for substance abuse issues to understand the details of what is covered under your plan.

Find out instantly if your insurance may be able to cover all or part of the cost of addiction treatment. Or call us today at You will speak with an admissions navigator, and they can help you through the verification process. American Addiction Centers can take the confusion out of contacting your insurance provider directly. Simply call us or fill in the form below and we can communicate with your insurance directly. By filling in our confidential form below, we can find out which treatment centers are in-network, we can advise you on the length of stay covered, and we can save you time and from the hassle of contacting your insurance company and/or looking through hard to understand insurance documents.

 

Insurance Providers and Rehab Coverage

Health insurance benefits are designed to make health care both affordable and accessible, and there’s no stigma attached to asking for help. People with addictions and insurance should use their coverage to the fullest in order to get the care they need to leave addictions behind for good. Talking to plan administrators is a great place to start, but remember that the staff of addiction treatment facilities can also be of vital help.

In some cases, they can smooth the path to payment, so families have one less thing to worry over as they recover. Explore more information about insurance providers and rehab coverage below. Call us at If you do not see your provider listed below, we may still be able to work with you or your insurance provider to find treatment.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Options With and Without Insurance

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Common Types of Healthcare Plans and Benefits

The three most common healthcare plans are health maintenance organization (HMO) plans, preferred provider organization (PPO) plans, and point of sale (POS) plans.4 Substance abuse treatment and recovery may be covered by your insurance provider. Learn more about which plan, HMO or PPO, offers the best coverage:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Cost and Coverage

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Estimated Time To Complete: ~2 mins

Who needs help? * 

We're here to help with your recovery. Please fill out this short form so our team can understand your needs.

 
 
 
 

 Myself 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 A family member or loved one 
 

 Next 
 

If you have any further questions about treatment, you may contact us directly at 888-966-8152 

 

Rehabs That May Accept This Insurance

American Addiction Centers (AAC) owns and operates facilities at various locations nationwide to provide you or your loved one addiction and mental health care.
 View All Facilities 
 

Filter By Location:

Select State 
 

 

Top Reasons to Choose American Addiction Centers

We know you have options when it comes to addiction treatment. That’s why American Addiction Centers goes above and beyond to help you achieve and maintain sobriety.

 
 

 
 

Frequently Asked Questions

How to bill insurance for substance abuse treatment?

People with addictions and insurance should use their coverage to the fullest in order to get the care they need. Talking to your insurance plan administrator by calling the number on the back of your insurance card is a great place to start. The staff of addiction treatment facilities can also be of vital help, easing the path to payment so families have one less thing to worry over as they recover.

How to get financial assistance for substance abuse treatment?

Financial assistance for substance abuse treatment can come from federal, state, and local governments. There are also grants available through SAMHSA, as well as insurance options for certain populations, such as veterans or low-income families. Read more .

What is the cost of outpatient substance abuse treatment?

Outpatient treatment for substance abuse can cover many different services and levels of care. It is recommended that patients work with their insurance provider and the rehab facility directly to determine their out-of-pocket expenses. Read more .

Can I Get or Change My Health Insurance Coverage After a Qualifying Life Event?

Certain life changes, referred to as qualifying life events, allow you to make changes to existing coverage or sign up for a new health insurance plan. These life changes include loss of health coverage, changes in your household, changes in your residence, and more. Qualifying life events include:

 

Losing existing job-based, individual, or student health coverage.

Losing eligibility for Medicare, Medicaid, or Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

Turning 26 years old and losing health coverage under your parent’s insurance plan.
Getting married or divorced.

Having a baby or adopting a child.

A death in your family that affects your health coverage.

Moving to a different zip code or county that changes your health plan area.

Moving as a student to or from the place where you will go to school.

Moving to or from the place where you live and work as a seasonal worker.

Moving to or from a shelter or other transitional housing.

Experiencing changes in your income that affect your qualifying coverage.

Gaining membership in a federally recognized tribe or status as an Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA) Corporation shareholder.

Becoming a U.S. citizen.

Leaving incarceration such as jail or prison.

Starting or ending service as an AmeriCorps member.

 

All situations are different, so these life events might require documentation—such as birth certificates, adoption records, marriage licenses, divorce papers, death certificates, rental agreements, or mortgages—to show that you have added family members, lost family members who provided the health coverage, or moved into a new health insurance plan area. These changes usually need to be done within 60 days of the life event, but check with you insurer on specifics.

Can I Have Multiple Insurance Providers?

Yes, you can have multiple insurance providers. When two (or more) health insurance plan providers cover the healthcare costs of one person, this is called coordination of benefits. You may have dual insurance coverage if:

 

You are married and covered under your insurance plan and your spouse’s.

You are under 26 years old and covered by your parents’ insurance and your own.

You are under 26 years old with divorced parents and are under both parents’ plan as a dependent.

You are over 65 years old and have coverage through your employer and Medicare.
If you have two separate health insurance plans, one plan will be your primary coverage and the other is your secondary coverage. Your primary provider pays first – up to its coverage limits. Your secondary insurance will then step in and pay the remaining cost (partial or full) of the treatment(s). Even after secondary insurance pays, you may still have some out-of-pocket costs.

		line-height: 44px;
	}
	h2 {
		font-family: var(--title-font);
		font-weight: bold;
		font-size: 30px;
		line-height: 40px;
		margin: 30px 0 24px;
	}
	h3 {
		font-family: var(--title-font);
		font-weight: bold;
		font-size: 24px;
		line-height: 32px;
		margin: 30px 0 24px;
	}
	h4 {
		font-family: var(--title-font);
		font-weight: bold;
		font-size: 20px;
		line-height: 32px;
	}

	p, span, a {
		font-family: var(--copy-font);
	}
	p {
		font-size: 16px;
		line-height: 25px;
		margin: 26px 0;
	}
	a {
		color: var(--interactive);
	}


	/* Same styles as p but can be used on <div>s */
	.bold{
		font-weight: bold;
	}
	.text-4xl {
		font-size: 36px;
		line-height: 44px;
	}
	.text-3xl {
		font-size: 30px;
		line-height: 40px;
	}
	.text-2xl {
		font-size: 24px;
		line-height: 32px;
	}
	.text-xl {
		font-size: 20px;
		line-height: 32px;
	}
	.text-lg {
		font-size: 18px;
		line-height: 28px;
	}
	.text-base {
		font-size: 16px;
		line-height: 24px;
	}
	.text-sm {
		font-size: 14px;
		line-height: 20px;
	}
	.text-xs {
		font-size: 12px;
		line-height: 16px;
	}
	.text-xxs {
		font-size: 10px;
		line-height: 16px;
	}









	button a {
		text-decoration: none;
		color: inherit;
	}
	blockquote {
		font-size: 23px;
		letter-spacing: -0.3px;
		line-height: 34px;
	}
	button {
		font-family: var(--copy-font);
		font-size: 12px;
		letter-spacing: 0;
		line-height: 15px;
		text-align: center;
		padding: 15px;
	}
	input, textarea {
		padding: 5px 10px;
		border: 1px solid var(--gray-300);
		display: block;
		font-size: 16px;
	}
	label {
		font-family: var(--copy-font);
		font-size: 14px;
		display: inline-block;
		padding-bottom: 5px;
	}
	iframe {
		max-width: 100%;
	}
	img {
		vertical-align: middle;
		max-width: 100%;
		height: auto;
	}
	ul, ol {
		padding: 0 0 0 20px;
		margin: 10px 0;
		line-height: 1.6;
	}

	ol li {
		padding-left: 10px;
	}

	hr {
		margin: 50px 0;
		border: 1px solid #f0f0f0;
	}

	.error-message {
		color: var(--error);
		padding-top: 10px;
		font-size: 13px;
		font-weight: bold;
	}

	/* Make clicks pass-through */
	#nprogress {
		pointer-events: none;
	}

	#nprogress .bar {
		background: #337fbd;

		position: fixed;
		z-index: 1031;
		top: 0;
		left: 0;

		width: 100%;
		height: 2px;
	}

	/* Fancy blur effect */
	#nprogress .peg {
		display: block;
		position: absolute;
		right: 0px;
		width: 100px;
		height: 100%;
		box-shadow: 0 0 10px #337fbd, 0 0 5px #337fbd;
		opacity: 1;

		-webkit-transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
		-ms-transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
		transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
	}

	/* Remove these to get rid of the spinner */
	#nprogress .spinner {
		display: block;
		position: fixed;
		z-index: 1031;
		top: 15px;
		right: 15px;
	}

	#nprogress .spinner-icon {
		width: 18px;
		height: 18px;
		box-sizing: border-box;

		border: solid 2px transparent;
		border-top-color: #29d;
		border-left-color: #29d;
		border-radius: 50%;

		-webkit-animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
		animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent {
		overflow: hidden;
		position: relative;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .spinner,
	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .bar {
		position: absolute;
	}
	.bcFloat,
	.widget-floating__teaser {
		display: none !important;
	}
	@-webkit-keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}
	@keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}

	/* font_awesome styles */
	.fa,.fab,.fal,.far,.fas {
		-moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
		-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
		display: inline-block;
		font-style: normal;
		font-variant: normal;
		text-rendering: auto;
		line-height: 1
	}
